title: Allow bootstrapping mTLS certificates outside of the helm chart
body:
## In what area(s)?
/area sentry

## Describe the proposal

### Help the operators read
This proposal is based on my production experience running Dapr (v1.13) via its Helm chart on a custom k8s cluster. The [documentation says](https://docs.dapr.io/operations/security/mtls/) (at the very top), you _can_ bring  your own certificates, but you might as well use the default ones.
> Dapr allows operators and developers to bring in their own certificates, or instead let Dapr automatically create and persist self-signed root and issuer certificates.

Not loving to read all the letters of a documentation for a feature you don't care for, you might as well stop reading right there. The sentence doesn't say you shouldn't let Dapr handle this for you unless you opt for a lovely cluster disaster one year later. Well, it does, but so subtly, one might tend to think of this being later handled somehow more gracefully (than ending up with the `dapr-operator` perpetually killing the dapr-enabled pods, for which the `dapr-injector` failed to inject the sidecar, because it saw an expired certificate in `dapr-sentry` 😮‍💨).
>If custom certificates have not been provided, Dapr automatically creates and persist self-signed certs **valid for one year**.

The warning about your inevitable downtime - if you use the default configuration - is [much farther in the docs](https://docs.dapr.io/operations/security/mtls/#updating-root-or-issuer-certs-using-kubectl).
> Avoiding downtime when rotating certificates
To avoid downtime when rotating expiring certificates your new certificates must be signed with the same private root key as the previous certificates. **This is not currently possible using self-signed certificates generated by Dapr.**

Correcting this by warning readers in the very 1st section should be pretty easy and it's my first mini-proposal.

### Make their life easier
Naturally, no one wants a production outage, so if there's a way, and it seems there is one pretty close, we should leverage it. When Dapr doesn't offer automatic root cert rotation, as a diligent operator you (finally) choose the other option - providing your own keys&certs. Because we all know that's a manual chore, we use the lovely [`cert-manager`](https://cert-manager.io/) k8s operator (greetings JoshVanL), which automatically creates and rotates TLS Secrets (which have these [deliberatley unmodifiable](https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/issues/675) keys `ca.crt`, `tls.key` and `tls.crt`). There you end up with a secret that's by default almost the same as the sentry's `dapr-trust-bundle` (with its keys `ca.crt`, `issuer.key` and `issuer.crt`). The only way to pass these values to the secret is via Helm chart values, unnecessarily dragging them out of the Secret already in k8s and passing them to Helm values.

I'd like to be able to skip this step and be able to set the name of the existing secret in helm values, in which case the `dapr-trust-bundle` wouldn't be managed by helm (or sentry) at all, but mounted the usual way to the sentry pod. The sentry `values.yaml` section could look like this:

```yaml
tls:
  existingSecret:
    name: my-trust-bundle
    caCert: ca.crt
    issuerKey: tls.key
    issuerCert: tls.crt
```

I see there's been some fairly recent movement on the TLS front, [fixing some original design inconsistencies for v1.14](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/5756) and [improving the default rotation seems to be also in the works](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/5958), however I don't see this particular use-case is being tackled yet.

comments.body: Ha, I finally found I'm not the first to come with a [cert-manager suggestion](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/3968), but that one seems kinda convoluted, while adding support for an independent external secret could be a matter of a couple changes in the helm chart.

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had activity in the last 60 days. It will be closed in the next 7 days unless it is tagged (pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved) or other activity occurs. Thank you for your contributions.

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had activity in the last 90 days. It will be closed in the next 7 days unless it is tagged (pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved) or other activity occurs. Thank you for your contributions.

+1 here 

And there is still another thing in Dapr Helm Chart which looks a bit hacky -> https://github.com/dapr/dapr/blob/v1.15.9/charts/dapr/charts/dapr_sentry/templates/dapr_sentry_deployment.yaml#L18-L31 there is a ConfigMap with just ca.crt that is loaded by multiple Dapr workloads as:

```
volumes:
  - name: dapr-trust-bundle
    configMap:
      name: dapr-trust-bundle
```
When they could just:
```
volumes:
  - name: dapr-trust-bundle
    secret:
      secretName: dapr-trust-bundle
      items:
        - key: ca.crt
          path: ca.crt
```

This same items trick could be used at sentry to rename trust-bundle keys.

> +1 here
> 
> And there is still another thing in Dapr Helm Chart which looks a bit hacky -> https://github.com/dapr/dapr/blob/v1.15.9/charts/dapr/charts/dapr_sentry/templates/dapr_sentry_deployment.yaml#L18-L31 there is a ConfigMap with just ca.crt that is loaded by multiple Dapr workloads as:
> 
> ```
> volumes:
>   - name: dapr-trust-bundle
>     configMap:
>       name: dapr-trust-bundle
> ```
> 
> When they could just:
> 
> ```
> volumes:
>   - name: dapr-trust-bundle
>     secret:
>       secretName: dapr-trust-bundle
>       items:
>         - key: ca.crt
>           path: ca.crt
> ```
> 
> This same items trick could be used at sentry to rename trust-bundle keys.

Forgot to mention about https://github.com/diagridio/dapr-cert-manager which I tried without any luck and it does either cover the Configmap stuff.

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had activity in the last 90 days. It will be closed in the next 7 days unless it is tagged (pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved) or other activity occurs. Thank you for your contributions.
not stale, still a needed feature

> Forgot to mention about https://github.com/diagridio/dapr-cert-manager which I tried without any luck and it does either cover the Configmap stuff.

I've been trying for some time now to get use the `dapr-cert-manager` with no success. It's likely due to it only updating the `dapr-trust-bundle` secret and not the configmap. 

Not quite sure, but the problem I have with it is that the once the `dapr-cert-manager` appends a renewed cert to the bundle, the Sentry service for some reason overwrites it with a newly generated one which results in a total failure.

This is a much needed feature for better automatic resilience.

> > Forgot to mention about https://github.com/diagridio/dapr-cert-manager which I tried without any luck and it does either cover the Configmap stuff.
> 
> I've been trying for some time now to get use the `dapr-cert-manager` with no success. It's likely due to it only updating the `dapr-trust-bundle` secret and not the configmap.
> 
> Not quite sure, but the problem I have with it is that the once the `dapr-cert-manager` appends a renewed cert to the bundle, the Sentry service for some reason overwrites it with a newly generated one which results in a total failure.
> 
> This is a much needed feature for better automatic resilience.

We did created our own controller to this this, it is really simple operator with very same logic than dapr-cert-manager and also based on cert-manager requirement. I will try to push to open source it because some people could find this useful

This is starting to get pretty ridiculous. _Everyone_ seems to be hacking around this with their custom solutions, while the fix might be a simple helm-chart modification. I don't understand why https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/3968 got more traction, but now it's getting stale all the same. While having a workaround makes me not need to contribute a fix anymore, but man would it be awesome if someone would just hack the original helm chart with a fix a publish it somewhere on GitHub (one can even make a helm repository out of it). Maybe then it would be possible to finally grab someone's attention to accept a PR based on that. 🙏 In the meantime, stay strong, you hackers! :)

Hello,
Sorry for delay opening this to the public. Here you got the code https://github.com/Elenpay/dapr-trustbundle
As a said the idea is barely the same as in https://github.com/diagridio/dapr-cert-manager but this one takes care of the ConfigMap the sidecars will use which is the missing part in that one.
My original plan was to fix in there but there no open issues nor any PR merged from outsiders in there.
The projects comes with Kustomize (I just tested this in simulated cluster) and a Helm Chart which we do use + a examples folder with the required part that Cert-Manager is supposed to do. Section https://github.com/Elenpay/dapr-trustbundle/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#usage-examples is more than enough to understand what this very very simple operator does.

Hope it can help more people out there!
